observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all funds from January to December 2024, along with the corresponding monthly bank statements for all depository accounts, to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the General Services Office (GSO) to:
Update the property records based on the results of the physical inventory; and
Reconcile with the accounting records to arrive at the reconciled balances of the Property, Plant, and Equipment accounts;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to address the deficiencies noted from the actual inventory count and disclose these in the Notes to the Financial Statements;
Submit the Journal Entry Vouchers, along with the Subsidiary Ledgers and any supporting documents, to recognize any loss of PPE from the concerned accountable officers or personnel, as well as any other necessary adjusting entries.
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team in the prescribed format; and
Remind the Inventory Committee to invite a representative(s) from the Commission on Audit to witness the physical count.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the journal entry vouchers affecting “Due from Other Funds” and “Due to Other Funds” accounts to identify the causes of the discrepancies; and
Prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Submit the Subsidiary Ledgers with the supporting documents of the following accounts: Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account, Loss of Assets, and Impairment Loss - PPE; and
Implement proper recordkeeping and internal control measures to ensure the accuracy and verifiability of all the financial data reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cashbooks for the 20% Development Fund; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain a separate set of accounting records and financial statements intended for this fund.

We recommended that the Chief Local Executive direct the BAC Secretariat and BAC Chairman to send written invitations or notices to the Commission on Audit at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings, as required by the government procurement act.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRM Officer to submit to the Audit Team the local sanggunian resolution declaring the Municipality under a state of calamity.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the LDRRM Officer to:
Prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP annually on its prescribed format; and
Prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format (Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team. The report shall be prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant-Designate.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain separate Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) for the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to expedite the recruitment process for filling vacancies in the Office of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management, as mandated by Section 12 (b) of RA 10121.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all collections are remitted and deposited within the prescribed period as required by the laws, rules, and regulations –
Daily or not later than the next banking;
At least once a week; or
As soon as the collections reach P10,000.

Require the non-member employees of the Philhealth to register or apply;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to update the list of employees and corresponding salaries in the Philhealth Premium List; and make the necessary adjusting entries to properly establish the amount due to Philhealth in the financial statement.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount to the non-member’s employees and officials whose Philhealth contributions were withheld.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit in full the monthly Philhealth contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the monthly and annual Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations (SAAOB) for both the current and continuing appropriations.

Submit to this office the list of all on-going government Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year,

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement for all of its depository accounts and submit them to the Office of the Auditor within fifteen (15) days after the end of the month to establish the adjusted balance of cash-in bank account every as of the month end; and
As signatories of the bank accounts, authorize the Municipal Accountant-Designate to enroll on e-access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer and witnessed by any representative of the Commission on Audit.
Require that the General Service Officer to maintain stock cards for each property, plant and equipment bearing property number while the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment.
Direct the General Services Officer to identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare necessary adjustments on deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
Require the Municipal Administrator to update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and other concerned personnel to determine the correct amounts of the unrecorded Land, Buildings, Construction and Heavy Equipment and Transportation Equipment to facilitate recording of the same in the books of accounts.
Require the concerned personnel to facilitate the processing of the TCT for all their untitled lots pursuant to Section 19 of PD 1445 to show conclusive proof of ownership of the LGU over the parcels of land.
Require the concerned personnel to refer to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 on the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs. The management should use all possible means to locate the one (1) unit of mini dump truck and the one (1) unit of tractor.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Prepare a correcting entry in order to recognize the completed infrastructure projects in the books of accounts;
Record in the Construction in Progress account the costs incurred in the construction of infrastructure projects. Upon completion, the CIP account shall be credited in order to recognize the appropriate PPE account; and
Attend trainings especially the seminar on the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, when available. Also, ensure strict compliance with the provisions of GAM for LGUs.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare a correcting entry as follows:

Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to transfer to the General Fund books the completed infrastructure assets and the equipment under Trust Fund

Recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate to compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties, to make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the computation, and provide yearly depreciation for all depreciable properties in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit list of DVs charged to  SEF in the total amount of ₱102,821.71, and to file separately the Special Education Fund’s disbursement vouchers (DVs) from General Fund’s DVs in order to facilitate audit of SEF ; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to indicate the fund source in the preparation of obligation request.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections intact or at least once a week or as soon as the collection reaches P10,000.00 to prevent possible loss or misappropriation of government funds, and to stop using collections for the purchase of accountable forms.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to transfer and deposit the proceeds of additional real property tax accruing to the municipality to SEF depository account, and to record transactions under SEF in a cashbook exclusively for SEF.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require all officials and employees concerned to prepare properly accomplished driver’s trip tickets and pre-approved before undertaking the trip.
Direct the General Services Officer to submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption of government motor transportation; strictly comply with the provisions of the Manual on Audit of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles as prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 to be properly guided in the documentation of fuel consumption and the use of government vehicles/equipment as well as to be able to regulate fuel consumption.
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure that payments for fuel expenses are duly supported with filled up driver’s trip tickets for proper evaluation on the propriety and reasonableness of fuel consumption before recording the same in the books of accounts.
Submit to the Office of the Auditor, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption not later than ten (10) days of the succeeding month for validation.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular no. 2009-006 in order to facilitate timely audit.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to:
Submit all disbursement vouchers with complete documentation to the Office of the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009;
To prevent further delay, ensure that submission of financial reports by the treasurer’s office and other accountable officers to the accounting office be made on time, complete and in order; and
Submit the lacking documents specified in Annex A immediately to the Office of the Auditor.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and other concerned personnel to submit lacking documents. All concerned personnel shall substantiate their claims with proper and complete documentation; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to ensure completeness and propriety of the supporting documents for every transactions before processing any payment pursuant to Section 4 of PD 1445. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension will be issued.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the GSO to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance, and attach duly accomplished Acceptance and Inspection Report to the Disbursement Voucher.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Hire a Certified Public Accountant, if plausible, and if not to hire or designate a personnel who has the competence, skill and experience both in the accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality for timely submission of the required accounting reports to the Office of the Auditor; and
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to stick to accounting functions in order to improve accounting operations wherein submission of required reports are prioritized, and
Appoint another employee as MENRO in order to allow the Municipal Accountant-Designate to focus only on accounting duties.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Strictly adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2003-004 on the prohibition of the Municipal Accountant and the personnel of the Office of the Municipal Accountant as regular member of the BAC, and
Require the BAC Members to attend trainings when available.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the BAC to send written invitations or notices to the mandated observers in all stages of the procurement, especially COA, at least five (5) calendar days before the date of the procurement stage/activity pursuant to Section 13.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Office of the Auditor the approved Loan Agreement including its supporting documents, and the supplemental budget for the receipt of loan proceeds amounting to ₱59,992,500.00.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the timeline of the submission of approved contracts and its supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. All approved contracts and its supporting documents should be submitted to the Office of the Auditor within five (5) working days from the execution of contract to facilitate timely review and evaluation of contracts, as well as the generation and communication of relevant audit results;
Require the BAC Secretariat to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the lacking documents enumerated in Annex C; and
Require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the requirements of the Revised IRR of R.A No. 9184 on the conduct of procurement activities.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the GAD Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2020 in its prescribed format including the Certificate of Review and Endorsement from the DILG-Provincial Office, and the GAD AR for CY 2020.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor all approved contracts together with its supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of contract to facilitate timely review and evaluation of contracts, as well as the generation and communication of relevant audit results; and to require the BAC Members to attend trainings when available.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to attach the lacking necessary documents as required by RA No. 11469 and other regulations.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the list of recipients of relief goods distributed.
Advise the BAC and Municipal Accountant-Designate to be well informed of rules and regulations under RA No. 11469 or Bayanihan to Heal as One Act and other GPPB Circulars issued by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB).

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Approved Program of Works with detailed engineering, Status of Work Accomplished validated by the LGU’s Inspectorate Committee, Inspection Report by the Municipal Engineer, pictures, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance, and other pertinent documents for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the LDRRM Officer to provide columns for the allotment class of   the LDRRMF;
Direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format (Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the Audit Team. The report shall be prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant-Designate; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAOs) for the LDRRMF as required by law.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned personnel to submit to the Audit Team the Approved SEF Budget for CY 2020.

c.	All disbursement should be issued with check direct to the name of the payees, concerned employees, suppliers and etc.

-operating expenses were still paid thru cash advance

As signatories of the bank accounts, authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll on e-access of their accounts so that bank statements could readily be available at any time.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
-Prepare and submit Report of Disbursements in the liquidation of her cash advances, and submit the same together with the supporting documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant, and
-Strictly comply with the timeline of the liquidation of cash advances for payroll, operating expenses and travel as provided in Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002.;
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to check completeness of liquidation documents before recording in the books of accounts as fully liquidated. Liquidations with insufficient supporting documents should not be received, but should be returned back to the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and/or concerned personnel for completion. If complete, submit the Report of Disbursements with all the vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Auditor within twenty (20) days from the accomplishment of the purpose pursuant to Section 1 of COA Circular 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012; and

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer to fast track the completion of updates on the assessment roll and the Real Property Tax Account Register/Certified List of Taxpayers, and submit the latter to the Office of the Municipal Accountant;
Based on the list, direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivable account to establish a realistic collection target on Real Property Tax. Also, the corresponding subsidiary ledger shall be maintained to monitor tax collections and delinquencies; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to develop an effective tax collection campaign and offering of incentives of full payment of tax dues in order to increase collection of real property taxes. For delinquent real property taxes, impose the remedies provided for in the Local Government Code.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Create an appraisal / inventory committee to conduct an actual physical inventory taking of all properties, plant and equipment headed by the General Services Officer to be witness by any representative of the Commission on Audit.
Require the General Service Officer should maintain stock cards of each property, plant and equipment bearing property number while the Municipal Accountant should maintain a subsidiary ledger card on each equipment.
Direct the General Services Officer to identify unserviceable properties and prepare an Inspection and Inventory Report for disposal.
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjustments on the deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and make reconciliation with inventory report, stock cards and subsidiary ledgers.
Require the Municipal Administrator to update the list of properties that need to be insured and submit the list to the GSIS.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the insurance premium of properties identified to be insured especially building to protect the interest of the government.
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the supplemental budget an additional allocation of fidelity bond in order that the property officer can apply for a bond.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate to compute the depreciation of all depreciable properties, to make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the computation, and provide yearly depreciation for all depreciable properties in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections once a week or as soon as the collection reaches P10,000.00 to prevent possible loss or misappropriation of government funds by the accountable officer, and
Direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to draw an adjusting entry: debit to Cash-Local Treasury and credit to appropriate income accounts at the same amount- ₱4,844.07.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to effect the immediate termination of its depository accounts in the PNB, Iligan Branch, and transfer the balance of the fund to any AGDBs located in the nearest area of jurisdiction, and  to refrain from opening new accounts with PNB;
Direct the Sangguniang Bayan Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the SB Resolution authorizing the closure of LGU-Pantar’s PNB accounts; and
To strictly follow the DOF Memorandum Order for safety, security and convenience of all the LGU.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Ensure strict compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advance pursuant to Section 89 of P.D No. 1445, and pertinent Sections of COA Circular No. 97-002;
Direct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that cash advances are supported with the necessary documents, and  additional cash advance for the same purpose shall be granted after previous cash advance is liquidated, or proper accounting thereof is made; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop the payment of reimbursements through his cash advance, reimbursement is discouraged as mode of payment for LGU’s expenses; and also payment to suppliers is made thru checks. And follow Republic Act 9184 in all procurement of goods.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Designate another permanent appointed official/employee as disbursing officer (DO), who shall also be properly bonded, in order to decrease the volume of cash advances being granted and handled by the Municipal Treasurer. Cash advance for payroll may be assigned to one DO, while cash advance for operating expenses, wherein payment by check may be very difficult, impractical, or impossible, may be assigned to another DO, and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide budget for the payment of  bond premium for the other Disbursing Officer.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to prepare and submit the monthly trial balance and Journal Entry Vouchers, and the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and submit on time the Statement of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations within the prescribed period for proper monitoring and timely decision making.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit the monthly required reports to the Office of the Municipal Accountant in order not to accumulate and submission maybe on a lump sum basis. Likewise, the audit team can update on the financial status of the Municipality, and
Require the National Offices such as the DBM, DILG, DSWD, DOLE and other offices to secure liquidation reports before transferring additional funds to the said LGU in order not to delay the implementation of their programs and projects and to avoid the possible misappropriation or losses or projects not implemented especially during transition period.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
If possible, designate a General Services Officer to handle the office inorder to organize the procurement process, submission of AIR and DR and the preparation of the Inventory Report.
Require the GSO Designate to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance, and attach duly accomplished Acceptance and Inspection Report to the Disbursement Voucher.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to hire a Certified Public Accountant, if plausible, and if not to hire or designate a personnel who has the competence, skill and experience both in the accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality for timely submission of the required accounting reports to the Office of the Auditor.

We have recommended to the BAC to send written invitations or notices to the mandated observers, especially COA, at least five (5) calendar days before the scheduled public biddings as provided in the IRR of RA 9184

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the BAC Secretariat to submit to the Office of the Auditor all approved contracts together with its supporting documents within five (5) working days from the execution of contract to facilitate timely review and evaluation of contracts, as well as the generation and communication of relevant audit results.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the remaining balance of the funds received from DBM and DILG to separate bank accounts with Authorized Government Depository Banks (AGDBs), and ensure succeeding fund transfers from National Government Agencies are deposited/downloaded to a separate bank account for easy monitoring of developments and utilization of the projects, and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit bank statements for CY 2019 for all of its bank/depository accounts.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Engineer to submit the Approved Program of Works with detailed engineering, Status of Work Accomplished validated by the LGU’s Inspectorate Committee, Inspection Report by the Municipal Engineer, pictures, Certificates of Completion and Acceptance, and other pertinent documents for every infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

We have recommended to the SB Secretariat to furnish copy of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) Resolution and the Declaration of State of Calamity to the Office of the Auditor as basis of the utilization of Quick Response Fund pursuant to Section 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the concerned personnel to submit a copy of Approved SEF Budget for CY 2019 to the Audit Team; and
Require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit the disbursement vouchers (DVs) amounting to ₱78,100.00 and other pertinent financial reports to the Office of the Auditor, and to strictly comply with the timeline on the submission of DVs together with the supporting documents and other financial reports to the Office of the Auditor.

b.	Limit cash advances to the specific purpose of which the cash advance was drawn.

d.	For procurement of goods and infrastructure, processing should be done on the procedures as mandated by Republic Act 9184.
e.	Disbursement through reimbursement are strongly discouraged except travelling expenses.
